definition: Shifts in cross-asset correlation regimes represent the deepest portfolio risk, as they invalidate diversification assumptions simultaneously across all asset classes. The 2022 stock-bond correlation flip provided empirical proof-of-concept.
evidence: 2022 simultaneous equity-bond drawdown as empirical proof
Historical analysis of correlation regime persistence and breaks
Portfolio-level impact analysis showing diversification failure during shifts
2022: SPX -25% and Bloomberg Agg -13% simultaneously — worst year for 60/40 since 1970s
VIX peaked at only ~37 in 2022 (less than half of COVID's 82) — correlation flip was the driver
Stock-bond correlation flip from -0.3 to +0.1-0.2 post-2022
Maturity-bifurcated: 2Y at -0.25 to -0.35 vs 30Y at +0.05 to +0.15
Mathematically certain for any diversified portfolio
open_questions: What leading indicators best predict correlation regime shifts?
How long do new correlation regimes persist once established?
Is the current stock-bond correlation regime stable or transitional?
Will the positive long-end correlation persist if inflation expectations re-anchor?
How should multi-asset portfolios optimally exploit the maturity bifurcation?
Is the bifurcation stable across different macro regimes?
